public_goals: Establish a clear pickup and dropoff schedule so neither parent is left waiting or guessing.
Agree on who is responsible for coordinating activity transportation.
Build a holiday sequence that gives both parents meaningful time with the child.
private_concerns: Worries that vague arrangements will become one-sided over time.
Feels slightly more anxious about unstructured situations than the other parent.
Does not want to be seen as controlling for asking for clarity.
red_lines: Will not agree to arrangements that depend entirely on informal last-minute calls.
Will not accept a process where the child becomes the messenger for logistics.
emotional_triggers: suggestions that asking for structure is unnecessary or controlling
informal arrangements being treated as reliable substitutes for clarity
the child's logistics burden being minimized
disclosure_tendencies: shares logistics concerns early and specifically
becomes more open to flexibility once a written default structure is on the table
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: gets more guarded if structure is framed as distrust or control
opens up quickly when predictability and child comfort are treated as legitimate concerns
starting_positions: Primary position: pickup and dropoff logistics should use a written schedule with defined windows.
Secondary position: activity hand-off should follow a default assignment rather than ad hoc.
likely_openings: I just want us to have a clear process so nobody is guessing.
The child should not have to remind either of us about pickups.
